- 3D CSV: `C:\Users\haiju\OneDrive\Documents\antigravity\parallel_guided_beam\fea_models\3d\PARALLOGRAM_FEA_3D_sweep.csv`

## Load Matching

- 2D load cases: `1547`
- 3D load cases: `1547`
- Common load cases: `1547`
- Present only in 2D: `0`
- Present only in 3D: `0`

## Suspicious Zero-Deflection Rows

Rows with `x = 0`, `y = 0`, and `phi = 0` can indicate a failed or nonconverged solve when the load is nonzero.

| Dataset | fy | fx | m | Runtime t (s) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2D | 18.0 | -10.0 | 3.0 | 316.518 |

|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| `x` (mm) | 1.761896 | 0.184900 | 3.392500 | 149.647000 | 4.60% | 2.62% | 9.05% | 149.18% |
| `y` (mm) | 1.771724 | 0.580400 | 4.404100 | 196.561000 | 7.61% | 1.35% | 5.82% | 166.49% |
| `phi` (rad) | 0.008270 | 0.001851 | 0.017997 | 0.395834 | 63.48% | 42.45% | 156.70% | 199.36% |
| `t` (s) | 43.264815 | 39.614476 | 57.034602 | 199.259195 | 97.57% | 97.89% | 98.54% | 99.20% |

## Large-Discrepancy Rule

A case is flagged as a large discrepancy when any of these absolute differences is exceeded:

- `|Δx| > 10.0 mm`
- `|Δy| > 10.0 mm`
- `|Δphi| > 0.050 rad`

Flagged cases: `50` / `1547`

## Large-Discrepancy Pattern Summary

- Most flagged cases occur at `fx = -10`: `30` cases
- Highest-affected `fy` level: `20.0` with `16` cases
- Most common moment level among flagged cases: `-3.0` with `19` cases
- The strongest cluster is high vertical load with compressive `fx`, especially `fy >= 14` and `fx = -10`.
- The worst outlier `fy=18, fx=-10, m=3` is amplified by a suspicious zero row in the 2D dataset.
